title: नरसिंह स्तुति (प्रह्लाद कृत)
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नरसिंह स्तुति - प्रह्लाद कृत ॥

श्रीप्रह्लाद उवाच -

ब्रह्मादयः सुरगणा मुनयोऽथ सिद्धाः
सत्त्वैकतानगतयो वचसां प्रवाहैः।
नाराधितुं पुरुगुणैरधुनापि पिप्रुः
किं तोष्टुमर्हति स मे हरिरुग्रजातेः॥१॥

मन्ये धनाभिजनरूपतपःश्रुतौजस्-
तेजःप्रभावबलपौरुषबुद्धियोगाः।
नाराधनाय हि भवन्ति परस्य पुंसो
भक्त्या तुतोष भगवान्गजयूथपाय॥२॥

नैवात्मनः प्रभुरयं निजलाभपूर्णो
मानं जनादविदुषः करुणो वृणीते।
यद्यज्जनो भगवते विदधीत मानं
तच्चात्मने प्रतिमुखस्य यथा मुखश्रीः॥३॥

तस्मादहं विगतविक्लव ईश्वरस्य
सर्वात्मना मुनिमनोनयनानुभावम्।
वन्दे परं प्रकृतिपूरुषयोः स्थितं यत्
नारायणं नरसिंहवपुषमाद्यम्॥४॥

बालस्य नेह शरणं पितरौ नृसिंह
नार्तस्य चागदमुदन्वति मज्जतो नौः।
तप्तस्य तत्प्रतिविधिर्य इहाञ्जसेष्टस्-
तावद्विभो तनुभृतां त्वदुपेक्षितानाम्॥५॥

yस्मादबिभ्यदपि मृत्युपतेः करालात्
तस्मादहं शरणमीश्वरं प्रपद्ये।
त्राहि त्राहि नरसिंह महानुभाव
दीनस्य मे शरणमन्यदतोऽस्ति नैव॥६॥

इति स्तुतः स भगवान्नरसिंहमूर्तिः
प्रह्लादमाश्लिष्य कृपार्द्रदृष्टिः।
मूर्ध्न्युपाघ्राय तदीयमुच्चैः
वरं ददौ प्रह्लदनायकोऽसौ॥७॥

॥ इति श्री भागवतपुराणान्तर्गत प्रह्लादकृत नरसिंह स्तुति सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narasimha Stuti - Prahlada Krita ||

Shri Prahlada Uvacha -

Brahmadayah Sura-ganah Munayo'tha Siddhah
Sattvaika-tana-gatayo Vachasam pravahah.
Naradhitum Puru-gunaih adhunapi pipruh
Kim toshtum-arhati sa me Harir-ugra-jateh.||1||

Manye Dhanabhijana-rupa-tapah-shrutaujas-
Tejah-prabhava-bala-paurusha-buddhi-yogah.
Naradhanaya hi bhavanti Parasya Pumsho
Bhaktya tutosa Bhagavan Gaja-yutha-paya.||2||

Naivatmanah prabhur-ayam nija-labha-purno
Manam janad-avidusah karuno vrinite.
Yad-yaj-jano Bhagavate vidadhita manam
Tach-chatmane pratimukhasya yatha mukha-shrih.||3||

Tasmad-aham vigata-viklava Ishwarasya
Sarvatmana Muni-mano-nayananu-bhavam.
Vande param Prakriti-purushayoh sthitam yat
Narayanam Narasimha-vapusham-adyam.||4||

Balasya neha sharanam pitarau Nrisimha
Nartasya chagadam-udanvati majjato nauh.
Taptasya tat-pratividhir ya ihanjaseshtah
Tavad-vibho tanu-bhritam tvad-upekshitanam.||5||

Yasmad-abibhyad-api Mrityu-pateh karalat
Tasmad-aham sharanam-Ishwaram prapadye.
Trahi trahi Narasimha Mahanubhava
Dinasya me sharanam-anyad-ato'sti naiva.||6||

Iti stutah sa Bhagavan Narasimha-murtih
Prahladam-ashlishya kripardr-drishtih.
Murdhny-upaghrayah tadiyam-uchchaih
Varam dadau Prahlada-nayako'sau.||7||

|| Iti Shri Bhagavata-Puranantargata Prahlada-krita Narasimha Stuti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: प्रह्लाद ने कहा - ब्रह्मा आदि देवगण, मुनि, सिद्ध अपनी वाणी से भी भगवान् की पूर्ण आराधना नहीं कर पाते, तो मैं अत्यन्त उग्र जाति का बालक कैसे प्रसन्न कर सकता हूँ? मैं मानता हूँ कि धन, कुल, रूप, तप, श्रुत, ओज, तेज, प्रभाव, बल, पराक्रम, बुद्धि, योग - ये सब परमपुरुष की आराधना के लिए नहीं हैं; भगवान् तो भक्ति से प्रसन्न हुए गजराज के लिए। हे नरसिंह, बालक के लिए माता-पिता ही शरण नहीं हैं, रोगी के लिए औषध नहीं है; जब तक आपकी उपेक्षा हो तब तक कुछ भी काम नहीं आता। रक्षा करो, रक्षा करो नरसिंह! मुझ दीन की कोई अन्य शरण नहीं।
benefits: Sacred prayer by Prahlada to Lord Narasimha from Bhagavata Purana
Teaches that pure bhakti alone pleases the Supreme Lord
Invokes fearlessness and complete divine protection
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears including fear of death
Deepens surrender and devotion to Lord Narasimha
description: Narasimha Stuti by Prahlada (नरसिंह स्तुति (प्रह्लाद कृत)) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Narasimha. Attributed to Srimad Bhagavata Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to grant fearlessness and complete divine protection and deepen surrender and devotion to Lord Narasimha. It is also said to remove all fears including fear of death. It is especially recommended on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. With 7 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Narasimha Stuti by Prahlada in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Prahlada's Narasimha Stuti from Bhagavata Purana in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer of surrender for divine protection.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Narasimha Stuti by Prahlada?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Stuti by Prahlada (नरसिंह स्तुति प्रह्लाद कृत) is the foremost Narasimha-stotra in the Vaishnava canon, recited by the boy-devotee Prahlada to pacify the ferocious Lord Narasimha after the slaying of the asura-king Hiranyakashipu (Prahlada's own father). The hymn is preserved in the Srimad Bhagavata Purana, Skandha 7, Adhyaya 9 (Bhagavata 7.9) — opening with the verse Brahmadayah Sura Ganah Munayo Atha Siddhah ('Even Brahma, the hosts of devas, the rishis, and the siddhas could not approach the angered Lord; only the child Prahlada dared to come forward and praise Him'). The stuti is one of the longest and most theologically rich bhakti hymns in the Bhagavata, containing ~50 verses elaborating Vishnu's supremacy, the futility of pride, and the supreme power of pure bhakti. (Source: Srimad Bhagavata Purana 7.9.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the story behind Prahlada's Narasimha Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Prahlada was the son of the asura-king Hiranyakashipu, who had obtained from Brahma a near-invincibility boon — he could not be killed by man or beast, by day or night, indoors or outdoors, on earth or in air, by any weapon. Hiranyakashipu demanded universal worship as god, but his five-year-old son Prahlada — already a profound devotee of Vishnu since hearing the dharma in his mother's womb from Sage Narada — refused to bow to anyone but Hari. After repeated tortures (poison, snake-pits, elephants, fire, sword, mountain-fall) failed to kill the child, an enraged Hiranyakashipu kicked a stone pillar and demanded 'Where is your Vishnu?' The pillar split open, and Vishnu emerged as Narasimha — the half-man half-lion form — at twilight (neither day nor night), placing Hiranyakashipu on His thigh (neither earth nor air) at the doorway threshold (neither indoors nor outdoors), and tore him apart with His claws (no weapon). When Narasimha remained terrifyingly angry, only Prahlada could approach Him, and offered this stuti. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 7.5–9.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Prahlada Narasimha Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Prahlada Narasimha Stuti is held to grant extraordinary spiritual and material protection: (1) fearlessness in the face of any threat — physical, psychic, or supernatural — since Narasimha is the supreme abhaya-mudra deity; (2) protection of children from accidents, illness, and hostile environments — the prayer of a child for a child; (3) destruction of enemies and removal of black magic, evil eye, and abhichara; (4) cultivation of pure bhakti — the stuti is praised by Madhvacharya, Ramanuja and Chaitanya as the gold standard of niskama bhakti (selfless devotion); (5) liberation from worldly attachments and the destruction of asuric tendencies in oneself; and (6) blessings for spiritual progress in adverse circumstances — Prahlada's example shows that bhakti thrives even when the family environment is hostile. (Sources: Srimad Bhagavata Purana 7.9; Vaishnava acharya commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Prahlada Narasimha Stuti be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Prahlada Narasimha Stuti is most powerfully recited on Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi — the appearance day of Narasimha, falling around April-May), every Chaturdashi tithi, every Saturday (Shanivar — to remove Shani afflictions), during Sandhya hours (twilight — recalling the avatara timing), on Janmashtami (since Narasimha is a Vishnu form), and especially when one feels surrounded by hostile influences or facing an enemy. Recommended practice: read the stuti from the Bhagavata Purana 7.9 once daily for 41 or 48 days as a parayana, ideally facing east, with a mustard-oil lamp and offerings of jaggery-water (panakam), buttermilk and panchamrita. Andhra Pradesh's Ahobilam Narasimha Kshetra and Maharashtra's Nira-Narasimhapur perform daily parayana of this stuti. (Sources: Narasimha Jayanti vrata vidhi; Ahobilam paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Prahlada considered one of the greatest devotees in Vaishnava tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Prahlada is enumerated in the Bhagavata Purana (7.10.43) as one of the twelve Mahabhagavatas — the supreme exemplars of pure devotion alongside Brahma, Narada, Shiva, the four Kumaras, Kapila, Manu, Janaka, Bhishma, Bali, Shukadeva and Yamaraja. His devotion is praised because (1) it was uncaused and natural (sahaja bhakti) — even from the womb; (2) it endured the most extreme persecution (a hostile asura father literally trying to kill him); (3) it sought nothing material — Prahlada explicitly refused boons offered by Narasimha and asked only for unwavering bhakti; (4) it was inclusive — he prayed for the liberation of his cruel father Hiranyakashipu and asked Narasimha to extend grace to the entire asura race; (5) it was universal — the stuti emphasizes that Vishnu sees everyone as equal, beyond caste, species, age, and birth. He embodies the Vaishnava ideal of niskama prema bhakti. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.5–10; 7.10.43; Vaishnava sampradaya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Narasimha Stuti compare to other Bhagavata Purana stutis?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhagavata Purana contains many great stutis — Brahma Stuti (BP 10.14, after the brahma-vimohana-lila), Devaki Stuti (BP 10.3, before Krishna's birth), Akrura Stuti (BP 10.40), Kunti Stuti (BP 1.8, after Mahabharata war), Bhishma Stuti (BP 1.9, on the bed of arrows), Mucukunda Stuti (BP 10.51), Gajendra Stuti (BP 8.3, by the elephant-king), Vena's son Stuti, and the Veda Stuti (BP 10.87). Among these, the Prahlada Narasimha Stuti is unique because (1) it is the longest single stuti in the Bhagavata (~50 verses); (2) it is offered by a child without parental guidance; (3) it expounds the highest Vaishnava philosophy combined with raw emotional surrender; (4) it pacifies the most fearsome form of Vishnu; and (5) it includes the prayer for universal liberation rather than personal benefit. Vaishnava acharyas across schools cite this stuti as the model for all subsequent Vishnu-bhakti compositions. (Sources: Srimad Bhagavata Purana various sargas; Vaishnava acharya commentaries.)
